cost.on_deploy: 5 indexes -> write amplification on cn_items/cn_events/cn_reviews/cn_lessons INSERT/UPDATE + storage; justified for a read-heavy app (reads O(N)->O(LIMIT); writes daily-cron batched). tie-breaker adds no cost.
cost.human_maintenance: index design + total-order tie-breaker + db_query_plan/determinism tool + verifier + adversarial review

value.stage: S7 UI/UX Motion & Performance (P03 data perf)
value.acceptance: PASS (verifier re-derives; 5 hot queries full-scan+sort -> index SEARCH no temp-btree, rows scanned O(N)->O(LIMIT); the tie-breaker makes cross-family ties deterministic (index==forced-scan, OFFSET pages disjoint); six-theme visual/motion contract byte-identical)
value.user_value: once deployed, the today/radar/browse/history/review pages + vitals sparkline stop full-scanning cn_items on every load (fast + stays fast as the corpus grows), and pagination/order is now deterministic (equal-recency items no longer shuffle across pages)
value.realized_on_deploy: indexes + deterministic order are source-complete + provably serve the queries; real D1 p95 + CWV p75 land after deploy + field traffic (NOT_DEPLOYED now)

unknown_not_zero: absolute D1 p95 (ms over the network) and CWV p75 are UNKNOWN until deployed + measured; the local-SQLite plan/determinism proof is representative of the planner behavior but not network latency. CWV 达标 NOT claimed.
